political.s: Alaska Air Group maintains a non-partisan PAC and contributes to both state and local candidates. The company engages in industry lobbying through trade associations like Airlines for America and the Chamber of Commerce.

charity.s: Alaska Airlines supports nonprofits through in-kind donations, sponsorships, and flight donations. The Alaska Airlines Foundation focuses on education, community development, and environmental preservation in Alaska and Hawaii.
charity.focus: Education, environmental sustainability, community development, diversity initiatives

environment.s: Alaska Airlines committed to net-zero carbon emissions by 2040 with a five-part strategy including operational efficiency, fleet modernization, sustainable aviation fuels, and carbon removal. The company reports 9% improvement in fuel efficiency in 2023.

labor.s: Alaska Airlines has faced multiple OSHA safety citations through contractors at Sea-Tac Airport and paid $45.2 million in penalties since 2000. However, the company has improved employee safety reporting by 26% since 2017 and was ranked top 10 safest airline in 2022.

dei.s: Alaska Airlines achieved gender parity on its independent board and has set measurable DEI goals including increasing leadership diversity and Black female pilots. The company links executive compensation to DEI metrics.

animals.s: Not applicable - airline services not related to animal testing.

guns.s: Not applicable - airline does not sell firearms.

privacy.s: Alaska Airlines' privacy practices are disclosed on their website with standard airline data collection. Limited public information available on specific data handling and protection standards.

execPay.s: CEO Ben Minicucci earned $8.6 million in 2024-2025, down from $10.3 million the prior year. Average employee salary approximately $130,000, resulting in a CEO-to-worker pay ratio of approximately 66:1.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
